extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) for all inputs. rec_drug_a is set true only by r4, which fires when qt_prolongation AND hypertension AND low_ejection_fraction AND g6pd_deficiency AND peptic_ulcer AND bradycardia are all true. rec_drug_b is set true only by r5, which fires whenever qt_prolongation is true. Since r4's condition includes qt_prolongation = true, any input satisfying r4 also satisfies r5. Each output is governed by a single rule (no conflicting rules, no priority/tie contention), and the defaults only apply to outputs left unset, so both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b become true together. The counterexample sets the six conjuncts of r4 to true (asthma/pregnancy/renal_impairment false), giving rec_drug_a=true (r4) and rec_drug_b=true (r5), which violates mutual exclusion.
